name: Bran Molasses Sunflower Bread
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup (1/2 stick) unsalted butter, at room temperature
1 1/2 cups Milk, scalded and cooled to lukewarm (95 F)
6 To 7 cups all-purpose flour or bread flour
recipeInstructions:
Makes 2 loaves
1. Crumble yeast into a small bowl. Stir in lukewarm water and sugar. Let stand until foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, add molasses and butter to milk and stir until butter melts.
3. Combine 2 cups all-purpose flour, bran, seeds, and salt in a large bowl. Whisk in yeast and milk mixtures until smooth, about 3 minutes.
4. Using a wooden spoon, mix in the remaining all-purpose flour (1/2 cup at a time) until dough forms a soft mass. Knead on a heavily floured surface until smooth and satiny, adding more flour if sticky.
5. Grease a large bowl. Add dough, turning to coat the entire surface. Cover with plastic wrap. Let rise in a warm, draft-free area until doubled, about 1 1/2 hours.
6. Grease two 9x5-inch loaf pans. Gently knead dough on a lightly floured surface until deflated. Cut into half. Pat each piece into a rectangle.
7. Roll up jelly roll fashion, pinching seams to seal. Place seam side down in prepared pans. Cover with towels and let rise in a warm, draft-free area until doubled in volume, about 45 minutes.
8. Position rack in the center of the oven and preheat to 375 F. Bake until loaves pull away from sides of pans, about 45 minutes. Immediately remove from pans. Cool completely on racks.
